firefox.name: PlainDate.withCalendar
firefox.notes: Calendars `islamic`, `islamic-rgsa` and `islamic-umalqura` are not available. See [bug 1950425](https://bugzil.la/1950425) and [bug 1954138](https://bugzil.la/1954138).

firefox.name: PlainDateTime.withCalendar

firefox.name: ZonedDateTime.withCalendar

firefox.name: AggregateError.serializable_object
firefox.notes: `AggregateError` serializes to the `AggregateError` type, with properties `name`, `message`, `cause`, and `errors`.

firefox.name: Error.serializable_object
notes: Version 103 serialized properties: `name`, `message`, `cause`, `fileName`, `lineNumber` and `columnNumber`.

Version 104 adds serialization of `stack` in the main thread ([`window.postMessage()`](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/API/Window/postMessage) and [`structuredClone()`](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/API/structuredClone)).

Version 110 adds serialization of `stack` in workers ([`worker.postMessage()`](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/API/Worker/postMessage) and [`structuredClone()`](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/API/structuredClone)).

firefox.name: EvalError.serializable_object
notes: Version 103 serializable properties: `name`, `message`, `cause`, `fileName`, `lineNumber` and `columnNumber`.

Version 104 also serializes `stack` in the main thread ([`window.postMessage()`](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/API/Window/postMessage) and [`structuredClone()`](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/API/structuredClone)).

Version 110 also serializes `stack` in workers ([`worker.postMessage()`](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/API/Worker/postMessage) and [`structuredClone()`](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/API/structuredClone)).

firefox.name: RangeError.serializable_object
notes: Version 103 serializable properties: `name`, `message`, `cause`, `fileName`, `lineNumber` and `columnNumber`.

Version 104 also serializes `stack` in the main thread ([`window.postMessage()`](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/API/Window/postMessage) and [`structuredClone()`](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/API/structuredClone)).

Version 110 also serializes `stack` in workers ([`worker.postMessage()`](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/API/Worker/postMessage) and [`structuredClone()`](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/API/structuredClone)).

Version 104 also serializes `stack` in the main thread ([`window.postMessage()`](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/API/Window/postMessage) and [`structuredClone()`](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/API/structuredClone)).

Version 110 also serializes `stack` in workers ([`worker.postMessage()`](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/API/Worker/postMessage) and [`structuredClone()`](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/API/structuredClone)).

Version 104 also serializes `stack` in the main thread ([`window.postMessage()`](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/API/Window/postMessage) and [`structuredClone()`](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/API/structuredClone)).

Version 110 also serializes `stack` in workers ([`worker.postMessage()`](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/API/Worker/postMessage) and [`structuredClone()`](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/API/structuredClone)).

notes: Version 103 serializable properties: `name`, `message`, `cause`, `fileName`, `lineNumber` and `columnNumber`.

Version 104 also serializes `stack` in the main thread ([`window.postMessage()`](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/API/Window/postMessage) and [`structuredClone()`](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/API/structuredClone)).

Version 110 also serializes `stack` in workers ([`worker.postMessage()`](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/API/Worker/postMessage) and [`structuredClone()`](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/API/structuredClone)).

firefox.name: URIError.serializable_object
notes: Version 103 serializable properties: `name`, `message`, `cause`, `fileName`, `lineNumber` and `columnNumber`.

Version 104 also serializes `stack` in the main thread ([`window.postMessage()`](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/API/Window/postMessage) and [`structuredClone()`](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/API/structuredClone)).

Version 110 also serializes `stack` in workers ([`worker.postMessage()`](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/API/Worker/postMessage) and [`structuredClone()`](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/API/structuredClone)).

firefox.name: statements.let
notes: Before Firefox 44, `let` is only available to code blocks in HTML wrapped in a `<script type="application/javascript;version=1.7">` block (or higher version) and has different semantics (e.g. no temporal dead zone).
Before Firefox 46, a `TypeError` is thrown on redeclaration instead of a `SyntaxError`.
Firefox 54 adds support of `let` in workers.

firefox.name: default_parameters.destructured_parameter_with_default_value_assignment
firefox.description: Destructured parameter with default value assignment

firefox.name: operators.destructuring
firefox.notes: Firefox provided a non-standard destructuring implementation from Firefox 2 to 40.

firefox.name: source.escaping
firefox.description: Line breaks and slashes are escaped

firefox.name: toString.escaping
firefox.description: Line breaks and slashes are escaped

firefox.name: TypedArray.indexOf
firefox.notes: Starting with Firefox 47, this method will no longer return `-0`. For example, `new Uint8Array([0]).indexOf(0, -0)` will now always return `+0`.

firefox.name: TypedArray.lastIndexOf
firefox.notes: Starting with Firefox 47, this method will no longer return `-0`. For example, `new Uint8Array([0]).lastIndexOf(0, -0)` will now always return `+0`.

firefox.name: statements.const
notes: Before Firefox 13, `const` is implemented, but re-assignment is not failing.
Before Firefox 46, a `TypeError` was thrown on redeclaration instead of a `SyntaxError`.

firefox.name: Promise.Promise
firefox.notes: Constructor requires a new operator since version 37.

firefox.name: operators.yield_star
firefox.notes: Starting with Firefox 33, the parsing of the `yield` expression has been updated to conform with the ES2015 specification.

firefox.name: default_parameters.parameters_without_defaults_after_default_parameters
firefox.description: Parameters without defaults after default parameters

firefox.name: operators.yield
notes: Starting with Firefox 33, the parsing of the `yield` expression has been updated to conform with the ES2015 specification.
Starting with Firefox 29, an `IteratorResult` object returned for completed generator function.

firefox.name: functions.arrow_functions
notes: The initial implementation of arrow functions in Firefox made them automatically strict. This has been changed as of Firefox 24. The use of `'use strict';` is now required.

Before Firefox 39, a line terminator (`\n`) was incorrectly allowed after arrow function arguments. This has been fixed to conform to the ES2015 specification and code like `() \n => {}` will now throw a `SyntaxError` in this and later versions.

firefox.name: parseInt.leading_zero_strings_as_decimal
firefox.description: Parses leading-zero strings as decimal, not octal

firefox.name: Map.size
firefox.notes: From Firefox 13 to Firefox 18, the `size` property was implemented as a `Map.prototype.size()` method, this has been changed to a property in later versions conform to the ECMAScript 2015 specification.

firefox.name: Set.size
firefox.notes: From Firefox 13 to Firefox 18, the `size` property was implemented as a `Set.prototype.size()` method, this has been changed to a property in later versions conform to the ECMAScript 2015 specification.

firefox.name: handler.ownKeys
firefox.notes: In Firefox 42, the implementation got updated to reflect the final ES2015 specification: The result is now checked if it is an array and if the array elements are either of type string or of type symbol. Enumerating duplicate own property names is not a failure anymore.

firefox.name: Array.@@iterator
firefox.notes: A placeholder property named `iterator` is used.

firefox.name: Iterator.@@iterator
firefox.notes: A placeholder property named `iterator` is used.

firefox.name: Map.@@iterator
firefox.notes: A placeholder property named `iterator` is used.

firefox.name: Set.@@iterator
firefox.notes: A placeholder property named `iterator` is used.

firefox.name: String.@@iterator
firefox.notes: A placeholder property named `iterator` is used.

firefox.name: TypedArray.@@iterator
firefox.notes: A placeholder property named `iterator` is used.

firefox.name: statements.for_of
firefox.notes: Before Firefox 51, using the `for...of` loop construct with the `const` keyword threw a `SyntaxError` ("missing = in const declaration").

firefox.name: ArrayBuffer.slice
firefox.notes: The non-standard `ArrayBuffer.slice()` method has been removed in Firefox 53 (but the standardized version `ArrayBuffer.prototype.slice()` is kept.

firefox.name: WeakMap.delete
firefox.notes: Before Firefox 38, this method threw a `TypeError` when the key parameter was not an object. This has been fixed in version 38 and later to return `false` as per the ES2015 standard.

firefox.name: WeakMap.get
firefox.notes: Before Firefox 38, this method threw a `TypeError` when the key parameter was not an object. However, the ES2015 specification specifies to return `undefined` instead. Furthermore, `WeakMap.prototype.get` accepted an optional second argument as a fallback value, which is not part of the standard. Both non-standard behaviors are removed in version 38 and higher.

firefox.name: WeakMap.has
firefox.notes: Before Firefox 38, this method threw a `TypeError` when the key parameter was not an object. This has been fixed in version 38 and later to return `false` as per the ES2015 standard.

firefox.name: WeakMap.set
firefox.notes: Before Firefox 38, this method threw a `TypeError` when the key parameter was not an object. This has been fixed in version 38 and later to return `false` as per the ES2015 standard.

firefox.name: Object.defineGetter
firefox.notes: Starting with Firefox 48, this method can no longer be called at the global scope without any object. A `TypeError` will be thrown otherwise. Previously, the global object was used in these cases automatically, but this is no longer the case.

firefox.name: Object.defineSetter
firefox.notes: Starting with Firefox 48, this method can no longer be called at the global scope without any object. A `TypeError` will be thrown otherwise. Previously, the global object was used in these cases automatically, but this is no longer the case.

firefox.name: String.anchor
firefox.notes: Starting with version 17, the quotation mark (") is replaced by its HTML reference character (`"`) in strings supplied for the `name` parameter.

firefox.description: Requires arguments to be `TrustedScript` instance when trusted types are enforced

safari.name: builtins.Atomics
safari.notes: Before Safari 16.4, `Atomics` is gated behind COOP/COEP. For more detail, read [Making your website "cross-origin isolated" using COOP and COEP](https://web.dev/coop-coep/).

safari.name: Error.cause
safari.notes: Default console logging for `Error` objects does not print the cause.

safari.name: await.top_level
safari.notes: Doesn't support multiple modules simultaneously importing a module containing a top-level await (see [bug 242740](https://webkit.org/b/242740)).

edge.name: toLocaleDateString.iana_time_zone_names
edge.description: IANA time zone names in `timeZone` option

edge.name: toLocaleString.iana_time_zone_names
edge.description: IANA time zone names in `timeZone` option

edge.name: toLocaleTimeString.iana_time_zone_names
edge.description: IANA time zone names in `timeZone` option

edge.name: name.inferred_names
edge.notes: Names for functions defined in a dictionary are properly assigned; however, anonymous functions defined on a var/let variable assignment have blank names.

edge.name: options_timeZone_parameter.iana_time_zones
edge.description: IANA time zone names in `options.timeZone` option

edge.name: default_parameters.destructured_parameter_with_default_value_assignment
edge.description: Destructured parameter with default value assignment

edge.name: default_parameters.parameters_without_defaults_after_default_parameters
edge.description: Parameters without defaults after default parameters

edge.name: sticky.anchored_sticky_flag
edge.description: Anchored sticky flag behavior per ES2015

edge.name: NumberFormat.format
edge.notes: Before Edge 18, numbers are rounded to 15 decimal digits. For example, `new Intl.NumberFormat('en-US').format(1000000000000005)` returns `"1,000,000,000,000,010"`.

edge.name: Number.toLocaleString
edge.notes: Before Edge 18, numbers are rounded to 15 decimal digits. For example, `(1000000000000005).toLocaleString('en-US')` returns `"1,000,000,000,000,010"`.

edge.name: source.escaping
edge.description: Line breaks and slashes are escaped

edge.name: toString.escaping
edge.description: Line breaks and slashes are escaped

edge.name: RegExp.unicode
edge.notes: Case folding is implemented in version 13

edge.name: builtins.Symbol
edge.notes: Edge 12 included Symbol properties in `JSON.stringify()` output.

edge.name: TypedArray.index_properties_not_consulting_prototype
edge.notes: Negative integers are not considered as indexed properties and therefore return the value of the prototype property.

edge.name: parseInt.leading_zero_strings_as_decimal
edge.description: Parses leading-zero strings as decimal, not octal

edge.name: statements.let
edge.notes: In Edge 12 and 13, `let` within a `for` loop initializer does not create a separate variable for each loop iteration as defined by ES2015. Instead, it behaves as though the loop were wrapped in a scoping block with the `let` immediately before the loop.
